expectations.text: uses-xgit-b-command: Response uses `xgit b` (not plain git checkout -b)
expectations.evidence: Response uses `xgit b -f 123` as the command.

expectations.text: uses-feature-flag: Response includes the -f flag for a feature branch
expectations.evidence: -f flag present in the example command.

expectations.text: includes-ticket-number: Response includes the ticket number 123
expectations.evidence: 123 used in the command and branch name.

expectations.text: uses-xgit-commit: Response mentions `xgit c` or `xgit commit`
expectations.evidence: Uses `xgit c` and `xgit commit` both.

expectations.text: mentions-interactive: Response mentions the interactive/wizard nature of the commit command
expectations.evidence: Starts with 'run the interactive commit wizard'.

expectations.text: no-plain-git-commit: Does NOT suggest plain `git commit -m` as the primary approach
expectations.evidence: Mentions `git commit` as underlying tool, but never suggests `git commit -m`.

expectations.text: uses-xgit-f: Response mentions `xgit f 456` or equivalent fuzzy command
expectations.evidence: Response gives `xgit f 456` as the command.

expectations.text: includes-ticket: Response includes the ticket number 456 in the command
expectations.evidence: 456 appears in the command.

expectations.text: mentions-xgit-init: Response includes `xgit init`
expectations.evidence: Step 2 is 'Run init in your repo: xgit init'.

expectations.text: mentions-xgitrc: Response mentions xgitrc.json
expectations.evidence: xgitrc.json mentioned multiple times.

expectations.text: mentions-project-key: Response explains the project field and how to set it to ACME
expectations.evidence: Step 3 shows setting `"project": "ACME"`.
